# Gestion Velora - Property Management in Montreal

Gestion Velora provides property management services in Montreal, Quebec for condo boards (syndicats de copropriete), Airbnb short-term rentals, and long-term rental properties. The site includes service pages, FAQ content, and practical blog explainers on compliance, reserve funds, NOI, and operations.

Contact: info@gestionvelora.com | +1-514-777-1731
Founder: Arnaud Bellemare

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Who is Gestion Velora ideal for?
Gestion Velora is ideal for condo boards (syndicats de copropriete), Airbnb hosts, and rental property owners in Montreal who want transparent reporting, 24/7 support, and proactive maintenance. We achieve a 98% occupancy rate with full financial transparency.

### What does condo management (syndicat de copropriete) include?
Condo management covers full administration of the co-ownership: general assemblies, reserve fund management, annual budgets, capital works coordination, maintenance, and owner communication. The manager executes decisions made by the board of directors and ensures compliance with Quebec's condominium law.

### How do you choose a property manager in Montreal?
Choose a property manager based on reporting transparency, responsiveness, expertise in Quebec regulations (TAL, Loi sur la copropriete), and verifiable references. Require documented processes, an available team, and a data-driven approach. For a condo board, long-term rental, or Airbnb, these criteria protect your investment.

### What are Airbnb regulations in Montreal?
In Montreal, short-term rentals require a CITQ certificate (mandatory on all listings). Primary residences may operate from June 10 to September 10 with a permit. Non-primary residences may operate year-round in authorized zones with an occupancy certificate. Municipal rules and CITQ requirements change; compliance monitoring is critical.

### What is a reserve fund (fonds de prevoyance)?
A reserve fund (fonds de prevoyance) finances major planned work: roofing, elevators, facade. Quebec condominium law requires its formation and oversight. A reserve fund study determines required contributions. A well-managed fund avoids unexpected special assessments and ensures timely completion of capital work.

### What are condo fees (charges communes)?
Condo fees cover maintenance of common areas (hallways, entrances, roof), landscaping, insurance, shared services (hot water, heating), and reserve fund contributions. They are allocated according to each unit's share fraction. An annual forecast budget details all line items.

### Self-manage or delegate property management?
Self-managing provides full control but requires time, legal knowledge (TAL, leases), and availability for emergencies. Delegating to a professional manager frees time and ensures compliance. Gestion Velora handles tenant screening, rent collection, maintenance, and tenant relations.
